nodes.title: Short title, commencement and application
nodes.summary: This section provides the short title of the Act, its commencement, and application.

nodes.title: Definitions
nodes.summary: This section defines various terms used throughout the Act.

nodes.title: General explanations
nodes.summary: This section provides general explanations applicable to the Act.

nodes.title: Punishments
nodes.summary: This section lists the types of punishments that offenders are liable to under the Act.

nodes.title: Commutation of sentence
nodes.summary: This section allows the government to commute sentences without the offender's consent.

nodes.title: Fractions of terms of punishment
nodes.summary: This section explains how fractions of terms of punishment are calculated.

nodes.title: Sentence may be (in certain cases of imprisonment) wholly or partly rigorous or simple
nodes.summary: This section allows the court to decide the nature of imprisonment.

nodes.title: Amount of fine, liability in default of payment of fine, etc.
nodes.summary: This section details the imposition of fines and consequences of default.

nodes.title: Limit of punishment of offence made up of several offences
nodes.summary: This section limits punishment when an offence comprises multiple offences.

nodes.title: Punishment of person guilty of one of several offences, judgment stating that it is doubtful of which
nodes.summary: This section addresses punishment when it is unclear which of several offences was committed.

nodes.title: Solitary confinement
nodes.summary: This section allows courts to order solitary confinement under certain conditions.

nodes.title: Limit of solitary confinement
nodes.summary: This section limits the duration and conditions of solitary confinement.

nodes.title: Enhanced punishment for certain offences after previous conviction
nodes.summary: This section provides for enhanced punishment for repeat offenders.

nodes.title: Act done by a person bound, or by mistake of fact believing himself bound, by law
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done under a mistaken belief of being bound by law.

nodes.title: Act of Judge when acting judicially
nodes.summary: This section exempts judges from liability for acts done in judicial capacity.

nodes.title: Act done pursuant to judgment or order of Court
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done in pursuance of a court order.

nodes.title: Act done by a person justified, or by mistake of fact believing himself justified, by law
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done under a mistaken belief of being justified by law.

nodes.title: Accident in doing a lawful act
nodes.summary: This section exempts accidental acts done without criminal intent.

nodes.title: Act likely to cause harm, but done without criminal intent, and to prevent other harm
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done to prevent greater harm without criminal intent.

nodes.title: Act of a child under seven years of age
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done by children under seven years of age.

nodes.title: Act of a child above seven and under twelve years of age of immature understanding
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done by children above seven and under twelve years of age who lack maturity.

nodes.title: Act of a person of unsound mind
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done by persons of unsound mind.

nodes.title: Act of a person incapable of judgment by reason of intoxication caused against his will
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done by persons intoxicated against their will.

nodes.title: Offence requiring a particular intent or knowledge committed by one who is intoxicated
nodes.summary: This section holds intoxicated persons liable as if they had the intent or knowledge required for an offence.

nodes.title: Act not intended and not known to be likely to cause death or grievous hurt, done by consent
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done with consent that are not intended to cause death or grievous hurt.

nodes.title: Act not intended to cause death, done by consent in good faith for person's benefit
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done in good faith for a person's benefit with their consent.

nodes.title: Act done in good faith for benefit of child or person of unsound mind, by, or by consent of guardian
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done in good faith for the benefit of a child or person of unsound mind with guardian consent.

nodes.title: Consent known to be given under fear or misconception
nodes.summary: This section invalidates consent given under fear or misconception.

nodes.title: Exclusion of acts which are offences independently of harm caused
nodes.summary: This section excludes acts that are offences regardless of harm caused.

nodes.title: Act done in good faith for benefit of a person without consent
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done in good faith for a person's benefit without their consent under certain circumstances.

nodes.title: Communication made in good faith
nodes.summary: This section exempts communications made in good faith for the benefit of the recipient.

nodes.title: Act to which a person is compelled by threats
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done under compulsion by threats of instant death.

nodes.title: Act causing slight harm
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts causing harm so slight that no reasonable person would complain.

nodes.title: Things done in private defence
nodes.summary: This section exempts acts done in the exercise of the right of private defence.

nodes.title: Right of private defence of body and of property
nodes.summary: This section establishes the right of private defence of body and property.

nodes.title: Right of private defence against act of a person of unsound mind, etc.
nodes.summary: This section extends the right of private defence against acts by persons of unsound mind.

nodes.title: Acts against which there is no right of private defence
nodes.summary: This section lists acts against which there is no right of private defence.

nodes.title: When right of private defence of body extends to causing death
nodes.summary: This section specifies when the right of private defence of body extends to causing death.

nodes.title: Selling child for purposes of prostitution, etc.
nodes.summary: This section criminalizes the sale or disposal of a child for prostitution or illicit intercourse, with a presumption of intent if the child is sold to a brothel.

nodes.title: Buying child for purposes of prostitution, etc.
nodes.summary: This section criminalizes the purchase or possession of a child for prostitution or illicit intercourse, with a presumption of intent if the child is obtained by a brothel.

nodes.title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
nodes.summary: This chapter covers various offences affecting the human body, including culpable homicide, murder, hurt, wrongful restraint, and kidnapping.

nodes.title: Culpable homicide.
nodes.summary: Defines culpable homicide as causing death with the intention or knowledge of likely causing death.

nodes.title: Murder.
nodes.summary: Defines murder as culpable homicide with specific intentions or knowledge, with exceptions for provocation, self-defense, and consent.

nodes.title: Culpable homicide by causing death of person other than person whose death was intended.
nodes.summary: Culpable homicide is committed if a person causes the death of someone other than the intended victim.

nodes.title: Punishment for murder.
nodes.summary: Prescribes death or life imprisonment for murder, with additional penalties for group crimes based on discrimination.

nodes.title: Punishment for murder by life-convict.
nodes.summary: Prescribes death or life imprisonment for murder committed by a person already serving a life sentence.

nodes.title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder.
nodes.summary: Prescribes imprisonment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder, with varying terms based on intent and knowledge.

nodes.title: Causing death by negligence.
nodes.summary: Punishes causing death by negligence, with specific penalties for medical practitioners and drivers.

nodes.title: Abetment of suicide of child or person of unsound mind.
nodes.summary: Punishes abetment of suicide by a child or person of unsound mind with death or life imprisonment.

nodes.title: Abetment of suicide.
nodes.summary: Punishes abetment of suicide with imprisonment up to ten years and a fine.

nodes.title: Attempt to murder.
nodes.summary: Punishes attempts to commit murder with imprisonment up to ten years or life, depending on the harm caused.

nodes.title: Attempt to commit culpable homicide.
nodes.summary: Punishes attempts to commit culpable homicide not amounting to murder with imprisonment up to three years.

nodes.title: Organised crime.
nodes.summary: Defines and punishes organized crime, including abetment and possession of crime proceeds.

nodes.title: Petty organised crime.
nodes.summary: Defines petty organized crime and prescribes imprisonment for such acts.

nodes.title: Terrorist act.
nodes.summary: Defines terrorist acts and prescribes severe penalties, including death or life imprisonment.

nodes.title: Hurt.
nodes.summary: Defines hurt as causing bodily pain, disease, or infirmity.

nodes.title: Voluntarily causing hurt.
nodes.summary: Punishes voluntary causing of hurt with imprisonment up to one year or fine.

nodes.title: Grievous hurt.
nodes.summary: Defines grievous hurt and lists specific injuries considered as such.

nodes.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt.
nodes.summary: Punishes voluntary causing of grievous hurt with imprisonment up to seven years.

nodes.title: Voluntarily causing hurt or grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means.
nodes.summary: Punishes causing hurt or grievous hurt using dangerous weapons with imprisonment.

nodes.title: Voluntarily causing hurt or grievous hurt to extort property, or to constrain to an illegal act.
nodes.summary: Punishes causing hurt to extort property or force illegal acts with imprisonment.

nodes.title: Voluntarily causing hurt or grievous hurt to extort confession, or to compel restoration of property.
nodes.summary: Punishes causing hurt to extort confessions or restore property with imprisonment.

nodes.title: Voluntarily causing hurt or grievous hurt to deter public servant from his duty.
nodes.summary: Punishes causing hurt to deter public servants from duty with imprisonment.

nodes.title: Voluntarily causing hurt or grievous hurt on provocation.
nodes.summary: Punishes causing hurt on provocation with imprisonment or fine.

nodes.title: Causing hurt by means of poison, etc., with intent to commit an offence.
nodes.summary: Punishes causing hurt using poison or drugs with intent to commit an offence.

nodes.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by use of acid, etc.
nodes.summary: Punishes causing grievous hurt using acid with severe imprisonment and fines.

nodes.title: Act endangering life or personal safety of others.
nodes.summary: Punishes acts endangering life or safety with imprisonment or fine.

nodes.title: Wrongful restraint.
nodes.summary: Defines wrongful restraint and prescribes punishment for obstructing a person's movement.

nodes.title: Wrongful confinement.
nodes.summary: Defines wrongful confinement and prescribes varying punishments based on duration and intent.

nodes.title: Force.
nodes.summary: Defines the use of force as causing motion or contact with another person.

nodes.title: Criminal force.
nodes.summary: Defines criminal force as using force to commit an offence or cause injury, fear, or annoyance.

nodes.title: Assault.
nodes.summary: Defines assault as gestures or preparations causing apprehension of criminal force.

nodes.title: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation.
nodes.summary: Punishes assault or criminal force without grave provocation with imprisonment or fine.

nodes.title: Assault or criminal force to deter public servant from discharge of his duty.
nodes.summary: Punishes assault on public servants to deter them from duty with imprisonment.

nodes.title: Assault or criminal force with intent to dishonour person, otherwise than on grave provocation.
nodes.summary: Punishes assault with intent to dishonour a person without grave provocation.

nodes.title: Assault or criminal force in attempt to commit theft of property carried by a person.
nodes.summary: Punishes assault during attempted theft of property carried by a person.

nodes.title: Assault or criminal force in attempt to wrongfully confine a person.
nodes.summary: Punishes assault during attempted wrongful confinement of a person.

nodes.title: Assault or criminal force on grave provocation.
nodes.summary: Punishes assault on grave provocation with simple imprisonment or fine.

nodes.title: Kidnapping.
nodes.summary: Defines kidnapping as taking a person beyond India or from lawful guardianship without consent.

nodes.title: Abduction.
nodes.summary: Defines abduction as compelling or inducing a person to go from any place by force or deceit.

nodes.title: Kidnapping or maiming a child for purposes of begging.
nodes.summary: Punishes kidnapping or maiming a child for begging with severe imprisonment and fines.

nodes.title: Negligent conduct with respect to animal
nodes.summary: Punishment for failing to guard against probable danger from animals.

nodes.title: Punishment for public nuisance in cases not otherwise provided for
nodes.summary: Fine for committing a public nuisance not covered by other sections.

nodes.title: Continuance of nuisance after injunction to discontinue
nodes.summary: Punishment for continuing a public nuisance after being ordered to stop.

nodes.title: Sale, etc., of obscene books, etc.
nodes.summary: Defines and punishes the sale and distribution of obscene materials.

nodes.title: Sale, etc., of obscene objects to child
nodes.summary: Punishment for selling obscene objects to children.

nodes.title: Obscene acts and songs
nodes.summary: Punishment for performing obscene acts or singing obscene songs in public.

nodes.title: Keeping lottery office
nodes.summary: Punishment for keeping an unauthorized lottery office.

nodes.title: OF OFFENCES RELATING TO RELIGION
nodes.summary: Covers offences that insult or damage religious sentiments or places.

nodes.title: Injuring or defiling place of worship with intent to insult religion of any class
nodes.summary: Punishment for damaging places of worship to insult a religion.

nodes.title: Deliberate and malicious acts, intended to outrage religious feelings of any class by insulting its religion or religious beliefs
nodes.summary: Punishment for insulting religious beliefs with intent to outrage feelings.

nodes.title: Disturbing religious assembly
nodes.summary: Punishment for causing disturbance to religious assemblies.

nodes.title: Trespassing on burial places, etc.
nodes.summary: Punishment for trespassing on burial places with intent to insult.

nodes.title: Uttering words, etc., with deliberate intent to wound religious feelings of any person
nodes.summary: Punishment for uttering words to wound religious feelings.

nodes.title: OF OFFENCES AGAINST PROPERTY
nodes.summary: Covers various offences related to theft, extortion, robbery, and property damage.

nodes.title: Theft
nodes.summary: Defines theft and provides punishment for committing theft.

nodes.title: Snatching
nodes.summary: Defines snatching as a form of theft and provides punishment.

nodes.title: Theft in a dwelling house, or means of transportation or place of worship, etc.
nodes.summary: Punishment for theft in specific locations like homes and places of worship.

nodes.title: Theft by clerk or servant of property in possession of master
nodes.summary: Punishment for theft by employees from their employers.

nodes.title: Theft after preparation made for causing death, hurt or restraint in order to committing of theft
nodes.summary: Punishment for theft involving preparation to cause harm.

nodes.title: Extortion
nodes.summary: Defines extortion and provides punishment for committing extortion.

nodes.title: Robbery
nodes.summary: Defines robbery and provides punishment for committing robbery.

nodes.title: Dacoity
nodes.summary: Defines dacoity and provides punishment for committing dacoity.

nodes.title: Robbery, or dacoity, with attempt to cause death or grievous hurt
nodes.summary: Punishment for robbery or dacoity involving attempts to cause serious harm.

nodes.title: Attempt to commit robbery or dacoity when armed with deadly weapon
nodes.summary: Punishment for attempting robbery or dacoity with a deadly weapon.

nodes.title: Punishment for belonging to gang of robbers, etc.
nodes.summary: Punishment for being part of a gang involved in theft or robbery.

nodes.title: Dishonest misappropriation of property
nodes.summary: Punishment for dishonestly misappropriating property.

nodes.title: Dishonest misappropriation of property possessed by deceased person at the time of his death
nodes.summary: Punishment for misappropriating property of a deceased person.

nodes.title: Criminal breach of trust
nodes.summary: Defines criminal breach of trust and provides punishment.

nodes.title: Stolen property
nodes.summary: Defines stolen property and provides punishment for handling it.

nodes.title: Cheating
nodes.summary: Defines cheating and provides punishment for committing it.

nodes.title: Cheating by personation
nodes.summary: Punishment for cheating by pretending to be someone else.

nodes.title: Dishonest or fraudulent removal or concealment of property to prevent distribution among creditors
nodes.summary: Punishment for hiding property to prevent its distribution among creditors.

nodes.title: Dishonestly or fraudulently preventing debt being available for creditors
nodes.summary: Punishment for preventing debts from being available to creditors.

nodes.title: Dishonest or fraudulent execution of deed of transfer containing false statement of consideration
nodes.summary: Punishment for executing a fraudulent deed of transfer.

nodes.title: Dishonest or fraudulent removal or concealment of property
nodes.summary: Punishment for removing or concealing property dishonestly.

nodes.title: Mischief
nodes.summary: Defines mischief and provides punishment for causing property damage.

nodes.title: Mischief by killing or maiming animal
nodes.summary: Punishment for causing harm to animals.

nodes.title: Mischief by injury, inundation, fire or explosive substance, etc.
nodes.summary: Punishment for causing damage through fire, explosives, or other means.

nodes.title: Mischief with intent to destroy or make unsafe a rail, aircraft, decked vessel or one of twenty tons burden
nodes.summary: Punishment for damaging transportation vehicles with intent to destroy or make unsafe.

nodes.title: Punishment for intentionally running vessel aground or ashore with intent to commit theft, etc.
nodes.summary: Punishment for intentionally grounding a vessel to commit theft.

nodes.title: Criminal trespass and house-trespass
nodes.summary: Defines criminal trespass and house-trespass and provides punishment.

nodes.title: House-trespass and house-breaking
nodes.summary: Defines house-breaking and provides punishment for committing it.

nodes.title: Punishment for house-trespass or house-breaking
nodes.summary: This section outlines the punishments for committing lurking house-trespass or house-breaking, with varying penalties based on the time of day and intent.

nodes.title: House-trespass in order to commit offence
nodes.summary: This section specifies the punishment for house-trespass committed with the intent to commit an offence, with penalties varying based on the severity of the intended offence.

nodes.title: House-trespass after preparation for hurt, assault or wrongful restraint
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for house-trespass committed after making preparations for causing hurt, assault, or wrongful restraint.

nodes.title: Dishonestly breaking open receptacle containing property
nodes.summary: This section deals with the punishment for dishonestly breaking open or unfastening a closed receptacle containing property.

nodes.title: OF OFFENCES RELATING TO DOCUMENTS AND TO PROPERTY MARKS
nodes.summary: This chapter covers offences related to the falsification of documents and property marks, including forgery and counterfeiting.

nodes.title: Making a false document
nodes.summary: This section defines what constitutes making a false document or electronic record, including various fraudulent acts.

nodes.title: Forgery
nodes.summary: This section outlines the punishment for making a false document or electronic record with intent to cause damage or commit fraud.

nodes.title: Forgery of record of Court or of identity card, etc.
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for forging documents purporting to be records of a court or identity documents issued by the government.

nodes.title: Forgery of valuable security, will, etc.
nodes.summary: This section deals with the punishment for forging documents that purport to be valuable securities, wills, or authorities to adopt.

nodes.title: Having possession of document described in section 337 or section 338, knowing it to be forged and intending to use it as genuine
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for possessing forged documents with the intent to use them as genuine.

nodes.title: Forged document or electronic record and using it as genuine
nodes.summary: This section defines a forged document or electronic record and prescribes punishment for using it as genuine.

nodes.title: Making or possessing counterfeit seal, etc., with intent to commit forgery punishable under section 338
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for making or possessing counterfeit seals or instruments with intent to commit forgery.

nodes.title: Counterfeiting device or mark used for authenticating documents described in section 338, or possessing counterfeit marked material
nodes.summary: This section deals with the punishment for counterfeiting devices or marks used for authenticating documents or possessing such counterfeit materials.

nodes.title: Fraudulent cancellation, destruction, etc., of will, authority to adopt, or valuable security
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for fraudulently cancelling, destroying, or defacing a will, authority to adopt, or valuable security.

nodes.title: Falsification of accounts
nodes.summary: This section deals with the punishment for falsifying accounts or making false entries with intent to defraud.

nodes.title: Property mark
nodes.summary: This section defines a property mark and prescribes punishment for using a false property mark.

nodes.title: Tampering with property mark with intent to cause injury
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for tampering with a property mark with intent to cause injury.

nodes.title: Counterfeiting a property mark
nodes.summary: This section deals with the punishment for counterfeiting a property mark used by another person or a public servant.

nodes.title: Making or possession of any instrument for counterfeiting a property mark
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for making or possessing instruments for counterfeiting a property mark.

nodes.title: Selling goods marked with a counterfeit property mark
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for selling goods with a counterfeit property mark.

nodes.title: Making a false mark upon any receptacle containing goods
nodes.summary: This section deals with the punishment for making a false mark on a receptacle containing goods to deceive others.

nodes.title: OF CRIMINAL INTIMIDATION, INSULT, ANNOYANCE, DEFAMATION, ETC.
nodes.summary: This chapter covers offences related to criminal intimidation, insult, annoyance, and defamation.

nodes.title: Criminal intimidation
nodes.summary: This section defines criminal intimidation and prescribes punishment for threatening another person with injury.

nodes.title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of peace
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for intentionally insulting someone with the intent to provoke a breach of peace.

nodes.title: Statements conducing to public mischief
nodes.summary: This section deals with the punishment for making statements that could cause public mischief or incite violence.

nodes.title: Act caused by inducing person to believe that he will be rendered an object of Divine displeasure
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for inducing someone to act by making them believe they will incur Divine displeasure.

nodes.title: Misconduct in public by a drunken person
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for misconduct in public by a person in a state of intoxication.

nodes.title: Defamation
nodes.summary: This section defines defamation and outlines exceptions where certain statements do not constitute defamation.

nodes.title: Breach of contract to attend on and supply wants of helpless person
nodes.summary: This section prescribes punishment for breaching a contract to attend to or supply the needs of a helpless person.

nodes.title: REPEAL AND SAVINGS
nodes.summary: This chapter discusses the repeal of the Indian Penal Code and the savings provisions related to its repeal.

nodes.title: Repeal and savings
nodes.summary: This section repeals the Indian Penal Code and outlines the savings provisions to preserve certain rights and liabilities.
